evidence: skillopt_sleep/__main__.py _add_common() (lines 68-93) enumerates all accepted flags, no --preferences entry exists, and parser.parse_args(argv) at line 323 is strict, not parse_known_args
description: Command doc tells the user to add --preferences to the sleep.sh invocation but the underlying python -m skillopt_sleep CLI has no such flag and errors with unrecognized arguments
suggested_fix: Remove the --preferences CLI-flag suggestion from the command doc and point users to the preferences key in ~/.skillopt-sleep/config.json instead, matching the codex and openclaw SKILL.md files

evidence: rc = os.system(" ".join(f'"{c}"' for c in cmd)) at line 107
description: Shells out via os.system instead of subprocess.run(list); current inputs are constrained (argparse choices, fixed paths) so not exploitable today, but the pattern is fragile against future unvalidated inputs
suggested_fix: Replace os.system(" ".join(...)) with subprocess.run(cmd) using the list form to avoid shell interpretation entirely

description: allowed-tools: Bash, Read (line 4) plus a bash invocation template driven by the raw $ARGUMENTS value (line 15) with no explicit instruction in the artifact to validate the argument against the documented action whitelist before executing
suggested_fix: Add an explicit instruction to validate $ARGUMENTS against the documented action list (status/dry-run/run/adopt/harvest/schedule/unschedule) before substituting it into the Bash invocation, rejecting anything else

description: DEEPSEEK_BASE_URL env var is used unchecked as the destination for a request carrying the DeepSeek API key in an Authorization header; mirrors a common SDK convenience pattern so may be intentional
suggested_fix: Validate DEEPSEEK_BASE_URL against an allow-list of expected hosts before attaching the Authorization header, or drop the override capability

evidence: all 7 core dependency lines (openai, pyyaml, numpy, openpyxl, azure-identity, azure-core, httpx) use only >= with no upper bound
description: All core dependencies are pinned with a lower bound only, allowing an unvetted future major release to install silently
suggested_fix: Add upper-bound caps (e.g. openai>=1.30.0,<2) or adopt a lockfile

evidence: directory listing of the repo confirms no skillopt-sleep-plugin directory exists anywhere; marketplace.json source.path is plugins/claude-code
description: README instructs `/plugin marketplace add ./skillopt-sleep-plugin` but that directory does not exist; the real plugin root per marketplace.json is plugins/claude-code
suggested_fix: Update the README install snippet to reference plugins/claude-code (or the actual marketplace repo layout) instead of the nonexistent ./skillopt-sleep-plugin path
